findings.summary: Required: exact trigger activation is not mechanically defined. Free-text trigger cells and overlapping source-line evidence cannot deterministically select or reject R/O activation. A redesign needs closed machine-readable trigger identity, explicit permitted co-activation, and deterministic selection/overlap proof.

Required: the claimed 98-route inventory still omits cross-leaf authority uses across intake/candidate/promotion, operation-definition schema/capability bindings, bootstrap request/response schema bindings, and the permanent published-Rust boundary. A complete 70-heading semantic re-audit is required after trigger identity is redesigned.

allowed_scope: Human decision between redesign and abandonment.

Do not run Continuation Review 3 or another automatic remediation.

Do not implement Rust/runtime/public API work, start HCM-0.5, rewrite historical evidence, or discard unrelated work.
acceptance_criteria: The user explicitly chooses redesign or abandonment.

stop_conditions: Stop if no explicit human choice is available.
Stop if a proposed continuation would execute decomposition or merely patch representative omissions.
Stop on any request to suppress Review 2 findings or exceed the exhausted continuation budget.
